UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores information about users of the OrdenaPro system, including their roles, department, and basic profile details. This entity assumes an external authentication system manages sensitive credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for communication and identification.

displayName.description: The full name or display name of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the system (e.g., 'Solicitante', 'Compras'), dictating their permissions.

department.description: The department the user belongs to within the organization.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

PurchaseRequest.title: PurchaseRequest
PurchaseRequest.description: Represents a request for a purchase made by a user from a specific department, awaiting review and approval by the purchasing team.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PurchaseRequest entity.

requesterId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this purchase request. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N PurchaseRequest)

requesterDepartment.description: The department of the user who made the request, for organizational tracking.

title.description: A short, descriptive title summarizing the purpose of the purchase request.

description.description: A detailed description providing context or justification for the purchase request.

itemIds.description: References to PurchaseRequestItem entities that detail the individual items requested. (Relationship: PurchaseRequest 1:N PurchaseRequestItem)

totalEstimatedCost.description: The sum of all estimated costs for items included in this request.

status.description: The current state of the purchase request (e.g., 'pendiente', 'aprobado', 'rechazado').

commentIds.description: References to PurchaseRequestComment entities providing feedback or notes on this request. (Relationship: PurchaseRequest 1:N PurchaseRequestComment)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the purchase request was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the purchase request was last updated.

approvedById.description: Reference to the UserProfile who approved this request. Only present if the status is 'aprobado'. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N PurchaseRequest)

approvedAt.description: Timestamp when the purchase request was approved. Only present if the status is 'aprobado'.

PurchaseRequestItem.title: PurchaseRequestItem
PurchaseRequestItem.description: Represents a single item detailed within a PurchaseRequest, including its requested quantity and estimated unit cost.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PurchaseRequestItem entity.

purchaseRequestId.description: Reference to the PurchaseRequest this item belongs to. (Relationship: PurchaseRequest 1:N PurchaseRequestItem)

name.description: The name or a brief description of the requested item.

quantity.description: The quantity of the item being requested.

estimatedCost.description: The estimated unit cost of the item in the local currency.

totalItemCost.description: The calculated total estimated cost for this specific item (quantity * estimatedCost).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this item was added to the purchase request.

PurchaseRequestComment.title: PurchaseRequestComment
PurchaseRequestComment.description: Represents a comment or note added to a PurchaseRequest, typically by a reviewer or the requester, to provide feedback or additional information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PurchaseRequestComment entity.

purchaseRequestId.description: Reference to the PurchaseRequest this comment is associated with. (Relationship: PurchaseRequest 1:N PurchaseRequestComment)

commenterId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who made this comment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N PurchaseRequestComment)

text.description: The actual content of the comment or note.

timestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the comment was made.

PurchaseOrder.title: PurchaseOrder
PurchaseOrder.description: Represents a formal purchase order generated automatically from an approved PurchaseRequest, detailing vendor information, final costs, and payment terms.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PurchaseOrder entity.

purchaseRequestId.description: Reference to the PurchaseRequest from which this Purchase Order was generated. (Relationship: PurchaseRequest 1:1 PurchaseOrder)

poNumber.description: A unique, human-readable purchase order number for tracking.

supplierName.description: The name of the supplier or vendor for this purchase order.

supplierContact.description: Contact information for the supplier (e.g., email address, phone number).

itemIds.description: References to PurchaseOrderItem entities that specify the items included in this order. (Relationship: PurchaseOrder 1:N PurchaseOrderItem)

totalCost.description: The total final agreed cost of all items in this purchase order.

currency.description: The currency in which the total cost is denominated (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'MXN').

status.description: The current status of the purchase order (e.g., 'generado', 'enviado', 'recibido', 'pagado', 'cancelado').

issuedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who issued or generated this purchase order. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N PurchaseOrder)

issuedAt.description: Timestamp when the purchase order was formally issued or generated.

expectedDeliveryDate.description: The anticipated date by which the ordered items are expected to be delivered.

paymentTerms.description: The agreed payment terms with the supplier (e.g., 'Net 30', 'Due on Receipt', 'Advance Payment').

signedPdfUrl.description: URL to the automatically generated, signed, and authorized PDF document of the purchase order.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the purchase order record was last updated.

PurchaseOrderItem.title: PurchaseOrderItem
PurchaseOrderItem.description: Represents a single item specified within a PurchaseOrder, including the final agreed quantity and unit cost.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PurchaseOrderItem entity.

purchaseOrderId.description: Reference to the PurchaseOrder this item belongs to. (Relationship: PurchaseOrder 1:N PurchaseOrderItem)

purchaseRequestItemId.description: Optional reference to the original PurchaseRequestItem from which this order item originated, for traceability. (Relationship: PurchaseRequestItem 1:1 PurchaseOrderItem)

name.description: The name or a brief description of the item being ordered.

quantity.description: The final quantity of the item ordered.

unitCost.description: The final agreed unit cost of the item.

totalItemCost.description: The calculated total cost for this specific ordered item (quantity * unitCost).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this item was added to the purchase order.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Each user can read/write their own profile. Includes 'role' and 'department' which are denormalized into related documents for authorization independence. A Cloud Function will maintain the /compras collection based on changes here.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Collection for explicit 'Compras' role assignment. Presence of a document signifies the user has the 'Compras' role. Used for fast role checks in security rules to avoid 'get()' calls.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user with the 'Compras' role.

definition.description: Top-level collection for purchase requests. Includes denormalized 'requesterId', 'requesterDepartment', and 'requesterRole' (from UserProfile) for authorization independence. The 'status' field ('pendiente', 'aprobado', 'rechazado') is crucial for workflow and rule conditions.

params.name: purchaseRequestId
params.description: The unique ID of the purchase request.

definition.description: Subcollection for items within a specific purchase request. Includes denormalized 'requesterId', 'requesterDepartment', and 'requesterRole' from the parent PurchaseRequest for authorization independence.

params.name: purchaseRequestId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent purchase request.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique ID of the purchase request item.

definition.description: Subcollection for comments on a specific purchase request. Includes denormalized 'requesterId', 'requesterDepartment', and 'requesterRole' from the parent PurchaseRequest for authorization independence.

params.name: purchaseRequestId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent purchase request.

params.name: commentId
params.description: The unique ID of the purchase request comment.

definition.description: Top-level collection for formal purchase orders generated from approved requests. Includes denormalized 'requesterId', 'requesterDepartment' (from the original PurchaseRequest) and 'issuedByUserId', 'issuedByUserRole' (from the issuing Compras UserProfile) for authorization independence.

params.name: purchaseOrderId
params.description: The unique ID of the purchase order.

definition.description: Subcollection for items within a specific purchase order. Includes denormalized 'requesterId', 'requesterDepartment' (from original PurchaseRequest), 'issuedByUserId', and 'issuedByUserRole' (from parent PurchaseOrder) for authorization independence.

params.name: purchaseOrderId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent purchase order.

params.name: poItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the purchase order item.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for OrdenaPro prioritizes Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure secure, scalable, and easily debuggable security rules. The core principle applied is denormalization of authorization-relevant data directly into each document, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules and enabling atomic operations.

1.  **User Profiles and Roles (`/userProfiles/{userId}`, `/compras/{userId}`):**
    *   `userProfiles` stores individual user data. Each user can manage their own profile. For explicit role-based authorization, a separate top-level `compras` collection is used. This DBAC (Database-Based Access Control) pattern allows for efficient `exists()` checks in security rules, verifying a user's role without requiring a `get()` on their user profile. When a user's role is updated in their `UserProfile`, a Cloud Function should update the corresponding document in the `/compras` collection, ensuring synchronization and authorization independence.

2.  **Purchase Requests (`/purchaseRequests/{purchaseRequestId}`):**
    *   `PurchaseRequest` documents are stored in a top-level collection. This collection's security posture is 'mixed' – private for the `requester`, and 'shared/editable' for users with the 'Compras' role. To achieve Authorization Independence, each `PurchaseRequest` document will denormalize `requesterId` (already present), `requesterDepartment` (already present), and `requesterRole` (new denormalized field from `UserProfile`). This allows rules to verify ownership (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.requesterId`) and role-based access without reading the `UserProfile` document.
    *   **QAPs Support:** For 'Solicitantes' to view their own requests, queries will filter by `requesterId`. For 'Compras' to view all requests, no specific filter is needed. This structure directly supports these query access patterns.

3.  **Purchase Request Items & Comments (`/purchaseRequests/{purchaseRequestId}/items/{itemId}`, `/purchaseRequests/{purchaseRequestId}/comments/{commentId}`):**
    *   These entities are nested as subcollections under their respective `PurchaseRequest`. This maintains logical hierarchy. For Authorization Independence, each `PurchaseRequestItem` and `PurchaseRequestComment` document will denormalize the `requesterId`, `requesterDepartment`, and `requesterRole` from its parent `PurchaseRequest`. This ensures that rules can determine access to items/comments based on the top-level request's ownership/roles without traversing the hierarchy via `get()`.

4.  **Purchase Orders (`/purchaseOrders/{purchaseOrderId}`):**
    *   `PurchaseOrder` documents are also in a top-level collection, reflecting their distinct lifecycle managed primarily by the 'Compras' department, but viewable by the original 'Solicitante'. Similar to `PurchaseRequest`, each `PurchaseOrder` document will denormalize `requesterId` (from the original `PurchaseRequest`), `requesterDepartment` (from the original `PurchaseRequest`), `issuedByUserId` (already present), and `issuedByUserRole` (new denormalized field from `UserProfile`). This allows rules to grant access based on either the original requester or the 'Compras' user who issued it, all within the document's own data.
    *   **QAPs Support:** 'Solicitantes' can query for their `PurchaseOrder` by `requesterId`. 'Compras' can list all `PurchaseOrders` directly. This is supported without additional rule complexity.

5.  **Purchase Order Items (`/purchaseOrders/{purchaseOrderId}/items/{poItemId}`):**
    *   Nested as a subcollection under `PurchaseOrder`. To ensure Authorization Independence, each `PurchaseOrderItem` document will denormalize `requesterId`, `requesterDepartment`, `issuedByUserId`, and `issuedByUserRole` from its parent `PurchaseOrder`.

This design leverages structural segregation (e.g., dedicated role collections) and radical denormalization of authorization context (`requesterId`, `requesterRole`, etc.) to create a robust and easily auditable security model. The `status` field is explicitly used for state modeling, simplifying queries and rule conditions.
